panels.description: Low angle wide shot: 'Screw-Gun Mules' and their 'Companions' (rugged human handlers), struggle to ascend a steep, rocky mountain path. Loose stones scatter under their hooves and boots. Dramatic, determined expressions on their faces. Overcast, dusty lighting, casting deep shadows that emphasize their arduous climb. The path is barely visible amidst rolling stones.

Medium shot: A 'Screw-Gun Mule' and a 'Companion' (handler) continue forward, having overcome a small obstacle. Their faces now show a mix of relief and renewed determination, a faint, weary smile on the handler's face. The harsh mountain terrain continues behind them, but the immediate path ahead appears momentarily clearer. Bright backlighting from a clearing sky hints at progress and their 'delight on a mountain height'.

Medium close-up shot: A 'Commissariat Camel' with expressive, frustrated eyes and a grumbling, open mouth. Its large, heavy pack is visibly sliding off its back. The dusty, uneven road and blurred figures of other camels in similar disarray are in the background. Harsh, midday sun accentuates the dust and fatigue, making the camel's disgruntled expression clear.

bubbles.text: We haven't a camelty tune of our own To help us trollop along, But every neck is a hair trombone And this our marching-song:
Can't! Don't! Sha'n't! Won't! Pass it along the line! Somebody's pack has slid from his back, Wish it were only mine! Somebody's load has tipped off in the road-- Cheer for a halt and a row!
Urr! Yarrh! Grr! Arrh! Somebody's catching it now!

panels.description: High angle wide shot: A vast, winding line of 'Children of the Camp' (various pack animals like mules and camels, accompanied by weary human handlers) stretches across an immense, desolate plain. The caravan appears endless, 'writhing and rolling far' towards a distant, hazy horizon. The ground is dry and cracked. A sense of solemn resolve and heavy destiny pervades the scene, bathed in a muted, somber light, emphasizing the scale of their march to war.

bubbles.text: Children of the Camp are we, Serving each in his degree; Children of the yoke and goad, Pack and harness, pad and load. See our line across the plain, Like a heel-rope bent again, Reaching, writhing, rolling far, Sweeping all away to war! While the men that walk beside, Dusty, silent, heavy-eyed, Cannot tell why we or they March and suffer day by day.
